verse_specific_anchors: the awake-to-X / asleep-to-Y inversion structure (yā niśā... tasyāṁ jāgarti / yasyāṁ jāgrati... sā niśā) is preserved verbatim — what the saṁyamī sees fully, others do not register; what others register fully, the muni does not see. No other verse in the Gita carries this specific double-inversion structure
the saṁyamī / muniḥ technical-term pair is named explicitly in the body of the analog; this term-pair is unique to 2.69 in the chapter

the same-scene-redirected-aperture framing ('Same scene; redirected aperture') is licensed by the verse's structure — the saṁyamī and ordinary beings perceive the same scene with different content, which is exactly Shankara's reading
objection: The analog could not equally apply to 2.55 (Arjuna's question about the sthita-prajña), 2.56 (the sage's stability), 2.57 (the sage who is unmoved by good and bad), or any other sthita-prajña block verse. The double-inversion (X-and-Y / Y-and-X) is specific to 2.69. The architecture-review-with-political-weather-vs-technical-truth framing is licensed by the inversion structure, not by general 'be wise in meetings' advice. Pass.
